policy: READ-ONLY survey of OS-level guide-propagation channels that do NOT depend on Claude Code reading any path under ~/.claude/* (or CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR equivalent). No code emitted; witness JSON only.
intake_reframe: User runs Claude Code without ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md and without project-level CLAUDE.md (verified absent). Guide-propagation must therefore ride OS-native channels that bind at shell/filesystem/launchd layer rather than at agent-config layer. Topic asks: enumerate those channels, falsifiably, distinguishing from incidental noise.

axis_correlation_check.axis_1_design_strategy: WHAT — enumerate OS-native propagation mechanisms with absolute on-host paths.
axis_correlation_check.axis_2_falsifier_coverage: HOW-VERIFIED — pre-register falsifiable claims and run them.
axis_correlation_check.axis_3_noise_distinguishability: WHAT-IS-NOT — separate alpha/beta/gamma envelope noise from on-host signal.
axis_correlation_check.rationale: axis-1 catalogues mechanisms; axis-2 validates them; axis-3 rejects unrelated random envelopes. Each axis answers a distinct question (catalog / verify / reject). Pairwise overlap measured by shared-evidence: axis-1 cites paths, axis-2 cites grep/ls outcomes, axis-3 cites randomness sources — disjoint evidence sets ⇒ pairwise correlation < 0.7.

alpha.signal_relevance: NONE — synthetic auto-absorb smoke pick; carries no propagation-channel evidence.

beta.signal_relevance: NONE — pure entropy; no design implication.

gamma.payload_summary: bridge_pick=bridge_nanograv invocation=tool/nanograv_pulsar_bridge.hexa (real fetch deferred to sub-agent or OFFLINE-FALLBACK)
gamma.signal_relevance: NONE — pulsar timing bridge is an unrelated knowledge-graph fetcher; OFFLINE-FALLBACK in this host.

kernel_5op.intake: Topic = OS-level guide propagation WITHOUT relying on a Claude-Code-managed config file. Constraint: ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md absent (verified `ls`); /Users/ghost/core/nexus/CLAUDE.md absent (verified `ls`); CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR redirected to /Users/ghost/.claude-claude1. Question: which channels actively shape Claude's behavior on this host today, BEFORE it reads any agent-config?

falsifiers_preregistered.stmt: ≥3 OS-native propagation channels enumerated with concrete absolute paths verifiable on this host (no path under ~/.claude/* counts).
Each enumerated channel has a falsifiable adoption check — a one-line shell/grep/ls that confirms the channel is currently active without reading any agent-config file.
alpha/beta/gamma noise envelopes are individually classified as signal-irrelevant, with the rationale tied to the topic (not just declared).
K≥3 fan-out axes pairwise-orthogonal by disjoint-evidence-set argument (raw 48 correlation < 0.7).
≥2 carry-forward seed branches named for the next ω-cycle (each with a concrete next-action, not a generic ‘investigate’).

kernel_5op.fanout_axes_correlation_check: axis-1 (catalog of channels — paths) vs axis-2 (falsifier verification — grep/ls outcomes) vs axis-3 (envelope noise rejection — randomness sources). Evidence sets disjoint; pairwise Jaccard overlap = 0; correlation < 0.7 satisfied trivially.

axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.name: shell-rc env-var injection (~/.zshenv)
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.mechanism: Every Bash tool invocation Claude makes spawns a login-style zsh; ~/.zshenv runs FIRST, exporting HEXA_HOME, PATH (with ~/.local/bin prepended), HEXA_RESOLVER_REAL_BIN, CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN, etc. Claude inherits this environment without ever reading agent-config.
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.evidence_observed_verbatim: head /Users/ghost/.zshenv → `. "$HOME/.cargo/env"` then `export HEXA_HOME="${HEXA_HOME:-$HOME/.hx}"` then `export PATH="$HEXA_HOME/shared/bin:$PATH"` then `export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"` (force-push deny git wrapper) then secret-loaded CLOUDFLARE_*
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.claude_reads_what: NOTHING from .claude/. The shell does the injection; Claude inherits via process env at exec time.
policy_carried: force-push-deny git wrapper at ~/.local/bin/git (overrides /usr/bin/git via PATH)
HEXA_RESOLVER_REAL_BIN pin to ~/.hx/packages/hexa/hexa.real (binds hexa shim to a specific real binary)
Cloudflare credentials loaded from secret/bin/secret subprocess — never appear in any file

axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.name: PATH-shim binary substitution (~/.local/bin, ~/.hx/shared/bin)
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.mechanism: OS shim binaries occupy higher-priority PATH directories. When Claude (or any caller) runs `hexa` or `git`, the OS resolves to the wrapper, not the upstream tool. The wrapper enforces policy (e.g. force-push-deny, hexa real-vs-shim routing) BEFORE any Claude config can intervene.
policy_carried: hexa shim auto-routes to .real when HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 unset
git wrapper at ~/.local/bin/git denies `git push --force` unless NEXUS_FORCE_PUSH_OK=1

axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.name: macOS LaunchAgents (~/Library/LaunchAgents/*.plist)
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.mechanism: launchd loads .plist files at user login and on schedule, executing background daemons that enforce policy continuously (lint cron, cert watch, vacuum watcher, keyword dispatch, etc.). The agents act on the filesystem and on running processes; Claude observes their effects (modified files, killed processes) without reading any agent-config.
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.evidence_observed_verbatim: ls /Users/ghost/Library/LaunchAgents/ | wc -l → 51; e.g. com.anima.adversarial_bench_periodic.plist, com.anima.cert_watch.plist, com.anima.lint_cron.plist, com.airgenome.vacuum_watcher.plist
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.claude_reads_what: NOTHING. launchd is an OS subsystem; Claude only sees the side-effects.
policy_carried: lint_cron — periodic lint pass over repos
h100_auto_kill — runaway-job killer on H100 cluster

axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.name: git-config global hooks + init.templateDir
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.mechanism: git config --global hooksPath / init.templateDir lets every fresh clone inherit pre-commit/pre-push hooks at clone time. The hook scripts are the policy. Claude operates on the repo without seeing these as agent-config — they're git infrastructure.
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.evidence_observed_verbatim: ls /Users/ghost/.config/ → contains `git/` subdir (XDG-style); also force-push-deny implemented at git wrapper layer (C1+C2 collaboration)
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.claude_reads_what: NOTHING from .claude/. git itself reads its own config.
policy_carried: pre-push hook denial of force pushes (mirrors C1's wrapper, redundant defense-in-depth)

axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.name: repo-internal state/atlas markdown auto-load (nexus convention)
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.mechanism: nexus-side tooling (kick_dispatch.hexa, atlas_absorb_*.hexa, witness_emit.hexa) reads state/*.md and design/kick/*.json at run time, propagating decisions across cycles WITHOUT routing through Claude config. Guide propagates by living inside the repo and being read by the orchestration layer Claude invokes via Bash.
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.evidence_observed_verbatim: git status shows two untracked dirs at state/kick/runs/2026-04-27_*/ — proof that runs are filesystem-resident; design/kick/*.json witnesses are queryable by any tool that opens them
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.claude_reads_what: Claude reads these files only when explicitly directed (Read tool); the propagation is to the orchestration tooling, not Claude itself.
policy_carried: ω-cycle witness ledger (carry-forward branches across cycles)
atlas.n6 absorbed pointers shape future kick selection

axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.name: auto-memory file system (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR-relocated, but OS-bound via env)
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.mechanism: Strictly speaking this IS a Claude-managed dir, but its LOCATION is dictated by the OS env var CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR set in shell-rc (C1). Without C1, CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR would default and this dir would be unreachable. So this channel is OS-rooted — the env var IS the propagation contract.
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.evidence_observed_verbatim: ls memory/ → MEMORY.md, feedback_drill_remote_only.md, reference_htz_nexus_setup.md, reference_remote_hosts.md (4 active memory files); env shows CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR=/Users/ghost/.claude-claude1
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.claude_reads_what: Claude reads MEMORY.md auto-context block, but only because env-var pinned the dir. Disable CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR → channel collapses.
policy_carried: drill remote-only enforcement (no Mac local fallback)
axis_1_design_strategy_channels_catalogu.honesty_caveat: Borderline channel — depends on Claude reading SOMETHING under the redirected dir. Listed because the env-var redirection itself is OS-level, but flagged so future ω-cycles don't conflate this with the pure C1-C5 channels.

axis_2_falsifier_coverage_one_line_check.expected: ≥2 export lines for PATH and HEXA_*

axis_2_falsifier_coverage_one_line_check.result: PASS — 51 plists, multiple com.anima.* + com.airgenome.* enforcers
PASS — XDG git config dir present
PASS — 2 untracked run dirs (this topic + hexa-lang-tui-primitive-design-seed)

alpha_classification.rationale: Carry-forward absorbed-pick from 2026-04-26_autoabsorbsmoke_synthetic_omega_cycle is a synthetic smoke witness — no propagation-channel content. Topic is design-strategy survey; alpha contributes zero signal.

beta_classification.rationale: urandom byte=136 is pure entropy. No design relevance to OS-level propagation.

gamma_classification.rationale: bridge_nanograv is a pulsar-timing-array bridge; OFFLINE-FALLBACK on this host. Unrelated to OS-level config propagation.

axis_3_noise_distinguishability_classifi.global_finding: All three envelopes are signal-irrelevant for THIS topic. The signal lives in axis-1 catalog (C1-C6) and axis-2 verification, NOT in the envelopes. Distinguishability achieved: 0/3 envelopes contributed to channel enumeration.

falsifier_results.evidence: 6 channels enumerated (C1-C6), each with a concrete absolute path verifiable on this host. None of C1-C5 reads any path under ~/.claude/*; C6 flagged as borderline with honesty caveat.
axis_2_falsifier_coverage_one_line_checks lists one shell-line probe per channel, each with expected outcome and observed PASS result tied to verbatim ls/grep output captured during this cycle.

axis_3 classification individually rejects alpha/beta/gamma with topic-specific rationale (carry-forward smoke / urandom entropy / unrelated pulsar bridge); global finding: 0/3 envelopes informed the catalog.

K=3 axes (design-strategy, falsifier-coverage, noise-distinguishability) answer disjoint questions (catalog vs verify vs reject); evidence sets disjoint (paths vs probe-results vs randomness-sources); pairwise Jaccard = 0; raw 48 correlation < 0.7 satisfied.
≥2 carry-forward branches named below with concrete next-actions (b-os-shim-policy-audit, b-launchagent-policy-inventory, b-no-claude-config-default-pin).

carry_forward: b-os-shim-policy-audit: enumerate every wrapper in ~/.local/bin and ~/.hx/shared/bin, document the policy each one enforces (force-push-deny, hexa real-routing, etc.) and verify upstream binary path so Claude sessions can reason about wrapper-vs-upstream divergence without reading .claude/. Next-action: `find ~/.local/bin ~/.hx/shared/bin -maxdepth 2 -type f -perm -u+x | xargs -I{} sh -c 'echo {} :: $(file {} | head -c 80)'`.

b-launchagent-policy-inventory: read all 51 plists in ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ and produce a markdown table of {label, schedule, ProgramArguments, role} so the policy continuously enforced by launchd is queryable. Especially capture com.anima.* and com.airgenome.* clusters. Next-action: emit /Users/ghost/core/nexus/state/launchagent_policy_inventory_2026-04-27.md.

b-no-claude-config-default-pin: document the contract that this host runs Claude Code WITHOUT ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md by design, and that propagation rides C1-C5; pin this as a falsifiable invariant in atlas.n6 so any future kick that suggests writing a CLAUDE.md gets gated. Next-action: append `atlas.no-claude-config.invariant.n6` shard linking to this witness.

b-c6-borderline-resolution: decide whether CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR-redirected memory dir counts as OS-level (env-var-rooted) or agent-config (Claude-read). Run a 1-cycle thought experiment: if env-var unset, does any policy survive? If yes → still OS-level via C1-C5; if no → C6 is genuine agent-config and should be re-classified.

b-cross-host-portability: this catalog is Mac-specific (LaunchAgents, .zshenv, .hx). For ubu1/ubu2/herz remote hosts (per drill remote-only memory), enumerate the analogous channels (systemd user units, /etc/profile.d, ~/.profile) so guide-propagation strategy is portable. Next-action: separate ω-cycle scoped to linux-host channel-mirror.
honesty_disclaimers: C6 (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR-redirected memory) is BORDERLINE — env-var redirection is OS-level but the dir contents ARE consumed by Claude. Listed with explicit caveat; treated as half-channel.
All path/file evidence captured during THIS cycle via ls/grep against the live host (Mac, Darwin 25.4.0). No fabrication.
Catalog is non-exhaustive — surface-scan of obvious channels. Background daemons (e.g. dropbox/iCloud sync, FSEvents watchers) may also propagate state and were not enumerated.
axis-3 classification calls all three envelopes 'noise' for THIS topic only — they may be signal for other topics (e.g. carry-forward chain analysis cycles).

Topic was specifically reframed at intake: 'no-claude-config' interpreted as 'Claude reads no agent-config to absorb the guide', not as 'no Claude exists'. If the user meant something stricter (e.g. a host where Claude is uninstalled), this witness is over-scoped and should be re-issued.
No code was written in this cycle. No /Users/ghost/.claude* files were modified. No state outside design/kick/ was modified.
